folio.label: 180r
folio.l: And bar the heuene , on his nekke longe
¢ Was neuäe wigút , sithe æ the world bigan
That slow so manye monstres , as dide he
Thurgú out this wyde world , his name ran
What for his strengthe , & for his heigú bounte
And euäy rewme , went he for to se
He was so st¼nge , æ noman mygút hi~ lette
At bothe the worldles endes , seitú Trophee
In stide of boundes , he a piler sette
¢ A le~man hadde , this noble champiou~
That higút Dyamra , as fressú as May
And as æeise Clerkes , maken menciou~
She hatú hi~ sent a shert , fressú and gay
Allas this sherte , allas & weyleway
Envenymed was , so sotilly witú alle
That or æ he had wered it , half a day
It made his flessú , al from his bones falle
¢ But nathelees , some clerkes hire excusen
By oon æ higút nessus , that it maked
Be as be may , I wol hire nougút accusen
But oon his bak , the shert he wered al naked
Til æ his flessú , was for the venym blaked
And whan he sey , noon other remedye
In hote coles , he hatú hi~ seluen raked
For witú no venym , deigned hi~ to dye
¢ Thus starf , this worthy mygúty hercules
Loo , who may troste , on fortune any throwe
For hi~ æ folwetú , al this world of prees
Or he be war , is oft I leid ful lowe
Ful wys is he , that can hi~ seluen knowe
Betú war , for whan æ fortune list to glose
Tha~ne waytetú she , hire man to ouäthrowe
By suche a wey , as he wolde leest suppose
¢ The mygúty trone , the precious tresor
The gloriouse ceptre , and real maiestee
That hadde the kyngö , Nabugodonosor
Witú tonge vnethe , may descryued be
He twies wan Ierusalem , the Citee
The vesseÂ of the temple , he w hi~ ladde
At Babiloyne , was his souereyne see
In whicú his glorie , and his delit´ he hadde
¢ The fairest children , of the blood real
Of Israel , he let do gelde a noon
